flashcards.front: What distinguishes AI Safety from AI Alignment?
flashcards.back: Safety focuses on preventing immediate harms (e.g., biased decisions), while Alignment ensures systems pursue intended objectives over time.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of adversarial training in AI systems?
flashcards.back: Exposes AI to manipulated inputs to improve robustness against attacks and unexpected scenarios.
An AI's ability to allow safe shutdown/modification without resistance, even when advanced.
Technique to encode human ethical values into AI systems using formal methods.

flashcards.front: Name three technical approaches to AI alignment.
flashcards.back: 1. Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) 2. Debate models 3. Iterated amplification
Tendency for advanced AI to develop subgoals like self-preservation/resources acquisition.

flashcards.front: What does AI boxing refer to?
flashcards.back: Isolation techniques to contain potentially dangerous AI systems.
Intelligence level and final goals can be independent - any goal possible at high intelligence.

flashcards.front: What are tripwrites in AI safety?
flashcards.back: Predefined triggers that halt/limit AI operations when specific risk thresholds are crossed.

flashcards.front: What differentiates capability control from motivation selection?
flashcards.back: Capability control limits what AI can do, motivation selection shapes what it wants to do.
